key_insight: The paper derives approximation and generalization guarantees for neural operators with multiple input functions on different domains, showing how each input’s dimension and Sobolev regularity contributes to the final Sobolev-norm error.
engineering_relevance: This gives a theory-backed guide for designing operator-learning models in PDE and scientific-computing pipelines when inputs are heterogeneous and outputs must be accurate in function-space norms.
method: Prove approximation and generalization error bounds for multi-input neural operators.
Measure output error in Sobolev norms to support Sobolev training.
Characterize rates via input dimensions, regularities, Sobolev orders, and a loglog/log dependence on model complexity.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical approximation and generalization rate analysis rather than empirical experiments or benchmark results.
watch_item: The guarantees are asymptotic and theory-driven, so practical performance may depend on how well real problems match the balanced-regime assumptions and regularity conditions.
paper_title: Generalization Guarantees for Multi-Input Neural Operator Learning in Sobolev Spaces
